# This script scrapes the most recent 90-day-median prices for a list of items from https://warframe.market using the warframe.market API.
# The script was created by 1Xanaton4You on 20.07.2026
# This is an open source script under GNU GENERAL PUBLIC LICENSE Version 3.
#
# Files needed to run the WarframeMarketScraperV2.py script:
#   ItemsFinder.py (in the same directory as this script)
#   warframe_market_database.py (is generated by ItemsFinder.py)
#
# I wrote this scrip to get a easier overview over the current prices on the warframe.market.
#
# How to use this script on first use:
# 1. On windows open a command prompt by typing cmd into the windows seach field.
# 2. Type "phyton" and press enter to check if you have phyton installed on your system.
#    2a. If you do not get something like this {Python 3.12.6 (tags/v3.12.6:a4a2d2b, Sep  6 2024, 20:11:23) [MSC v.1940 64 bit (AMD64)] on win32
#                                               Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.}
#        you first have to install Phyton on your system (https://www.python.org/downloads/).
#    2b. Make sure that the "requests" packet is installed in your Python enviroment. To check this type in "python -m pip list".
#        If the list shows something like this {requests           2.34.2} it is installed. If it is not installed type in 
#        "python -m pip install requests" to install it.
#    2c. If you have a Phyton version and the requests packet installed type exit() and press enter to leave the phyton prompt.
# 3. Alter the [Set this according to your needs] part of the sript according to your needs, in case you have added new lists in ItemFinder.py.
# If you have already done steps 1 to 3 at least once start at step 4:
# 4. Start the script by double clicking the WarframeMarketScraperV2.py
# 5. Select if you want to scan the warframe market for selleable items and store the results into "warframe_market_database.py" (y=yes, n=no).
# 6. Select if you want to scrape all defined item lists (y) or select a specific list (n). Choose the list number, if you want to scrape a single list.
# 7. Wait for the results. You can save the scraped results in a CSV-file when the scraping is done, if you choose to do so.
#    The CSV-files will be created in the folder this script runs from.
